evidence_quotes.quote: The video game industry is infamously known for being one of the industries with some of the worst anti-consumer practices currently available in the market.

paragraphs.text: I strongly believe that the Digital Fairness Act should take into account the industry of video games in order to improve it as a whole from the consumer's side. The video game industry is infamously known for being one of the industries with some of the worst anti-consumer practices currently available in the market. This industry greatly hurts the consumer while it empowers the publishers, companies and organizations, granting them almost complete control over the consumer. Although, this can happen in any type of video games. This problem becomes specially evident with video games where their main focus is about playing in any kind of online mode. In those situations, if the online mode is managed by the video game developer company itself, which is often the case. If the company suddenly bankrupts or decides to stop supporting the game, the online mode for the video game effectively shuts down and stops being functional. In the worst case, which is when we are dealing with a video game that is 100% online based without offline content. The video game and product then becomes non-usable without any chances to ever work again. As you can image, this puts the consumer in a very unfair and uncertain position as they are completely at the mercy of the company. Keep in mind, that we are talking about a product that the consumers have rightfully paid for. The consumers could enjoy their video game for many years to come or abruptly come to and end the next month after they make their purchase. The consumers do not know that nor cannot know that. Apart from this example that I have provided, the are many more situations and cases like this out there. I believe that it is already time that the consumers get some power back. The balance has been tipped for so long in the power and favour of the organizations. It is time once again to balance the scale back to a more neutral and favourable position towards the consumers like it was many years ago.
